design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed environments, defeat enemies using absurdist weapons and power-ups, rescue objective (Bessie the pig) while managing consumable side effects
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management through food/alcohol with mechanical consequences (gut meter causing noise, drunk meter causing loss of control) that force players to balance healing against tactical disadvantage
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Hillbilly protagonist fighting alien invasion through rural American locations (trailer parks, meat packing plants) with irreverent tone and nonsensical weaponry
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately absurdist parody of both FPS genre conventions and rural American stereotypes; mechanics tied to thematic humor (flatulence mechanics, intoxication affecting controls) rather than pure balance; refusal to separate mechanical consequence from narrative/comedic effect

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through consumable management strategy; players can choose to carry moon pies, pork rinds, or alcohol, each with distinct trade-offs (noise generation vs intoxication vs automatic consumption). Moonshine power-up allows temporary reset of negative meters, creating tactical decisions around when to use high-risk consumables.

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection system with three main consumable types (moon pies, pork rinds, alcohol) plus special power-ups (moonshine for speed boost with meter reset). Weapons appear to be environment-pickup based rather than upgrade trees.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression through themed environments (meat packing plant, trailer park, etc.) with linear advancement toward rescue objective and alien invasion confrontation
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer deathmatch modes (documented in expansion and limited versions)
Multiple consumable load-out strategies affecting playstyle
Expansion packs with new levels and locations (Route 66, Area 69 desert)
Sequel with new environments, vehicles, and weapons

world_building_techniques: Environment-based storytelling through location design (fictional Hickston, Arkansas; rural Americana aesthetic)
Character-driven humor through protagonist voice and mechanics (flatulence, drunkenness)
Thematic enemy design (evil clones of neighbors, alien invaders, male/female alien variants)
Psychobilly/cowpunk soundtrack reinforcing hillbilly parody aesthetic

boss_design.philosophy: Thematically coherent absurdist bosses that reinforce parody tone rather than mechanical complexity. Progression from mini-boss (Assface) to final confrontation (Queen Vixen, alien invasion leader).
notable_bosses: Queen Vixen (alien invasion leader, final boss)

faction_indicators: Human protagonists (Leonard and Bubba brothers) vs alien invaders
Evil clones of neighbors (corrupted humans under alien influence)
Thematic location ownership (meat packing plant, trailer park, brothel in sequel)
danger_signals: Environmental hazards tied to rural decay (trailer parks, swamps)
environmental_storytelling: Fictional Arkansas town (Hickston) as primary setting
Meat packing plant as industrial decay marker
Trailer parks representing rural economic struggle
Sequel locations: jackalope farms, Disgraceland, riverboats, brothels, Area 69 desert (escalating absurdism)
